abstract: Code quality guidelines for shipping production-grade software. Twenty rules across seven categories covering error handling, security, testing, architecture, documentation, naming, and code structure. Each rule includes incorrect/correct code examples. Derived from analysis of 470+ real-world PRs, 304K AI-authored commits, and industry security research. Use when writing, reviewing, or refactoring code to avoid common AI-generated code pitfalls: 1.7x more bugs, 2.74x more security issues, 20-30% verbosity.
